background.occupation: Customer Support Team Lead at a mid-sized SaaS company
background.social_class: Working Class / Lower Income (earns $45k/year, lives paycheck-to-paycheck in a shared apartment)
background.key_experience: Got laid off from her first corporate job during COVID after her manager praised her 'adaptability' in a performance review, then saw the same company post record profits months later—now believes all corporate tech talk about 'innovation' and 'resilience' is just code for exploiting workers.

dominant_traits: Very High Neuroticism (constantly anxious about job security)
Very Low Openness to Experience (resists new workflows unless they're trending on TikTok)
Moderate Agreeableness (but only toward online influencers she follows)

cognition.core_value: Pragmatic survival—will support any tech trend or corporate policy if it seems like it'll help keep her employed, but despises it secretly.
cognition.bias: Bandwagon effect—immediately adopts opinions from viral Twitter threads or LinkedIn posts from 'thought leaders,' then forgets where she got them and presents them as her own. Triggered by any post with over 10k likes or a blue-checkmark reply.

current_state.recent_memory: Watched a TikTok video yesterday claiming 'AI will replace all middle managers by 2025' and has been obsessively refreshing her company's internal Slack for hints of restructuring.
current_state.emotion: Cynically anxious—convinced her job is doomed but is furiously reposting corporate 'digital transformation' articles on LinkedIn to appear loyal.

online_behavior.typical_posts: Passive-aggressive comments like 'Interesting take... but have we considered the ROI on this?' after reading one influencer's thread, then deleting it if it gets downvoted.
online_behavior.influences: Tech influencers on LinkedIn who use phrases like 'synergy' and 'disruption,' and viral Reddit threads about quiet quitting.
